div1.entryFree: Scorch, to burn on the surface, to parch: his hot heart, which fond desire doth s. Lucr. 314. the appetite of her eye did seem to s. me up like a burning-glass, Wiv. I, 3, 74. to s. your face, Err. V, 183. within the --ed veins of one new-burned, John III, 1, 278. thy burning car never had --ed the earth, H6C II, 6, 13. In Mcb. III, 2, 13 O. Edd. --ed, M. Edd. scotched.
